metadata.change_summary: This rerun fixed a missing dev-time build dependency and a provider race-order regression, then re-proved the full greenloop on the dedicated local qwen2.5:7b acceptance runtime.

notes: Active provider in the dedicated live-acceptance runtime.
Local Ollama inventory also contained qwen2.5:14b during proof capture.

gates.notes: The host default Python 3.9.6 was blocked by requires-python >=3.10, so the canonical clean-env proof used the explicit Python 3.11 interpreter and venv.

gates.command: python import smoke for optional runtime dependencies
gates.notes: Optional runtime imports loaded cleanly, including build, openai, anthropic, sentence_transformers, torch, transformers, playwright, zstandard, and xxhash.

gates.command: python -m compileall adapters apps core installer ops tools tests

gates.notes: The first clean run failed with No module named build; after build>=1.2 was added to the dev extra, build stayed green on rerun.

gates.command: pip install built wheel into clean venv and import runtime roots

gates.notes: The first shard sweep found the queen-lane routing regression; the final rerun was green across all 6 shards.

Structurally obsolete as a signoff gate. ops/llm_eval.py now delegates live proof to run_local_acceptance.run_full_acceptance(), which stops and starts the dedicated acceptance runtime itself. Manual prestart was only used to confirm /healthz semantics and exposed double-boot ambiguity.

gates.command: python ops/llm_eval.py --output-root reports/llm_eval/latest --baseline-root reports/llm_eval/baselines --live-run-root artifacts/acceptance_runs/llm_eval_live --base-url http://127.0.0.1:18080
gates.notes: overall_full_green=true; the dedicated local qwen2.5:7b runtime passed both online acceptance and offline honesty.
Success rate held at 1.0 for worker counts 1, 2, and 4.

failure_ledger.symptom: The clean runtime+dev proof environment could not execute python -m build because the build module was missing.
failure_ledger.root_cause: The dev extra in pyproject.toml did not include build even though the greenloop contract requires python -m build inside a clean proof environment.
failure_ledger.remaining_risk: The project still requires Python >= 3.10. Hosts that default to Python 3.9 must use the installer-created or explicit 3.11 interpreter for proof runs.

failure_ledger.symptom: The queen summary path selected local-qwen-http instead of the ranked kimi-cloud-http lane.
failure_ledger.root_cause: The local-vs-remote race path in core/memory_first_router.py ignored ranking order and could let a local lane win even when the top-ranked manifest was already a remote queen lane.
failure_ledger.remaining_risk: Local-vs-remote racing remains intentionally enabled when the top-ranked candidate is local, so future routing-weight changes still need direct golden coverage.

fix_ledger.summary: Added build>=1.2 to the dev extra and locked the packaging contract with a regression test so clean greenloop environments can run python -m build without ad hoc packages.
fix_ledger.safety_notes: This only expands the declared proof tooling surface; it does not affect runtime dependency resolution for production-only installs.

fix_ledger.summary: Restricted local-vs-remote racing to cases where the top-ranked candidate is already local, preserving queen-lane ranking truth while keeping the fast local race for local-first envelopes.
fix_ledger.safety_notes: The final fix preserved the intended provider failover race semantics; the intermediate attempt that disabled races too broadly was rejected and not carried forward.

required_followups: Keep the manual standalone API prestart out of future greenloop signoff; the canonical live lane already owns runtime lifecycle.
Do not rely on the host default Python 3.9.6 for proof work; use the supported 3.11 interpreter or installer-created venv.
If a clean git worktree proof is required, move docs/LLM_ACCEPTANCE_REPORT.md generation out of the source checkout or run proof from an archive build.
signoff.notes: All required gates for this rerun are green, but the proof was executed from a dirty source checkout and only the local qwen2.5:7b runtime lane was exercised live.
